provenance.source: NASA Earth Fact Sheet (mean volumetric radius)
provenance.note: radius is the mean; Earth is an oblate spheroid (equatorial 6378.1 km, polar 6356.8 km) — sphere rendering is a knowing simplification

render_approximations.what: orbit drawn circular at semi-major axis, in the ecliptic plane
render_approximations.why: v0 keeps the first canvas readable; eccentricity and inclination are real and already recorded above, just not yet rendered
render_approximations.correction_path: render full Keplerian elements (rung 2), then swap to JPL Horizons ephemerides
render_approximations.expected_error: up to ~21,000 km positional (eccentricity term) — labeled, not hidden

provenance.note: random points for depth perception — NOT real star positions. Correction path: Hipparcos/Gaia bright-star catalog.
fixed directional light standing in for the Sun; no Sun object exists yet (rung 3)
